steps.title: Identify what the conclusion requires
steps.content: The researchers concluded that the Casarabe people developed "a form of urbanism in the Amazon basin." Urbanism means organized, concentrated settlement with significant infrastructure — cities, districts, roads, canals. Any finding that supports this conclusion must show evidence of exactly that kind of dense, coordinated development.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice against the urbanism standard
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'They show shapes consistent with widely separated settlements of roughly equal small size surrounded by uncultivated jungle.': Widely separated, equally small settlements with jungle between them describe sparse distribution — the old view the researchers are contradicting. ✗
  • Choice B — 'They show shapes consistent with long-distance footpaths running from Casarabe territories to large cities outside the region inhabited by the Casarabe people.': These large cities are outside Casarabe territory, so they provide no evidence of Casarabe urbanism. ✗
  • Choice C — 'They show shapes consistent with scattered small farms created by clearing jungle areas near sources of fresh water.': Scattered small farms are rural, not urban. This supports neither density nor coordinated infrastructure. ✗
  • Choice D — 'They show shapes consistent with monumental platforms and dense central settlements linked to smaller settlements by a system of canals and roadways.': Monumental platforms, dense central settlements, and an integrated network of canals and roads are precisely what urbanism looks like — cities connected to surrounding communities by planned infrastructure. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
